concept: Tactile / Perceivable or Distinguished Through Touch
description: Perceivable, readable, or distinguishable through touch. A tactile format may use shape, texture, movement, pressure, or position to carry information. 'Palo' (touch) names contact; 'palethu' says touch can do the relevant perceptual work.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'pa' and liquid 'le' echo the contact and continuation of 'palo' (touch). Breathy 'thu' ends with the feel of air and surface passing beneath a hand.
grammatical_notes: A predicative or attributive adjective: 'ha phelotu palethu nai.' (This format is tactile.) It can modify a marker, surface, signal, map, or communication practice. The quality-noun rule supplies tactility.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Raised line, carved edge, woven change, and warm material let form speak to the hand as deliberately as color speaks to the eye.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The adjective gives touch equal standing as a way to receive information rather than treating it as a lesser copy of sight.

tags.aesthetic: form and texture encountered through touch
tags.physical: touch as the relevant sensory channel
